snippets.title: A minimal singly-linked list
snippets.code:
from __future__ import annotations
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional

@dataclass
class Node:
    val: object
    next: Optional['Node'] = None

def walk(head: Optional[Node]):
    while head:
        yield head.val
        head = head.next

# Build A -> B -> C
head = Node('A', Node('B', Node('C')))
print(list(walk(head)))   # ['A', 'B', 'C']
snippets.explanation: Two fields per node and a `next` pointer is all you need; iteration is a while-loop.

snippets.title: Prepend (O(1)) and find (O(n))
snippets.explanation: Prepending creates a new node pointing to the old head — constant time. Search walks the chain — linear time.

snippets.title: deque is a doubly-linked list
snippets.code:
from collections import deque

buf: deque[str] = deque(maxlen=3)
buf.append('a')
buf.append('b')
buf.append('c')
buf.append('d')   # 'a' is evicted because of maxlen
print(list(buf))   # ['b', 'c', 'd']

buf.appendleft('Z')
print(list(buf))   # ['Z', 'b', 'c'] — d was evicted on the right
snippets.explanation: maxlen turns a deque into a fixed-size sliding window in one line.

snippets.title: OrderedDict as an LRU cache
snippets.explanation: OrderedDict's doubly-linked list makes move-to-end and pop-from-front both O(1) — the canonical LRU implementation.
